Name: Beedrill V
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each of your Beedrill V in play. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hisuian Voltorb
Effects: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only during your first turn. Choose up to 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: An enigmatic Pokémon that happens to bear a resemblance to a Poké Ball. When excited, it discharges the electric current it has stored in its belly, then lets out a great, uproarious laugh.

Name: Hisuian Electrode
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
Flavor-Text: The tissue on the surface of its body is curiously similar in composition to an Apricorn. When irritated, this Pokémon lets loose an electric current equal to 20 lightning bolts.

Name: Scyther
Flavor-Text: The large, wickedly sharp scythes on its forearms are truly fearsome weapons. Prey’s attempts to flee are unfailingly thwarted by this Pokémon’s nimble motions.

Name: Scyther
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The large, wickedly sharp scythes on its forearms are truly fearsome weapons. Prey’s attempts to flee are unfailingly thwarted by this Pokémon’s nimble motions.

Name: Yanma
Flavor-Text: Its frail wings are so thin that one can see clear through them. However, during flight these wings exhibit the power to churn air with force enough to launch a house skyward.

Name: Yanmega
Effects: If you have more cards in your hand than your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.

Name: Heracross
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has an unparalleled horn. Heracross itself demonstrates tremendous power—it’s capable of throwing several people trained in the traditional arts of war at once.

Name: Kricketot
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When its antennae hit each other, it sounds like the music of a xylophone.

Name: Kricketune
Ability-Effect: Your [G] Pokémon in play, except any Kricketune, get +40 HP. You can’t apply more than 1 Swelling Tune Ability at a time.
Flavor-Text: It uses its cutlass-like arms to produce sound, the melody of which varies from individual to individual. It is a worthwhile endeavor to seek out one’s favorite tunes.

Name: Combee
Effects: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: The members of the trio spend all their time together. Each one has a slightly different taste in nectar.

Name: Vespiquen
Effects: Reveal any number of Sweet Honey cards from your hand. This attack does 60 damage for each card you revealed in this way.
Flavor-Text: It skillfully commands its grubs in battles with its enemies. The grubs are willing to risk their lives to defend Vespiquen.

Name: Leafeon
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards of different types and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Cells similar to those of plants have been found in its fur. Its hard tail can fell a large tree with one stroke, and the tail’s sharpness exceeds even that of a sword crafted by a master.

Name: Shaymin
Effects: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only during your first turn. Search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: When the turning of seasons brings the cruel winter to its end and the joyous people give thanks to the heavens, Shaymin appears and covers the withered land with flowers.

Name: Petilil
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The leaves on its head are highly valued for medicinal purposes. Dry the leaves in the sun, boil them, and then drink the bitter decoction for remarkably effective relief from fatigue.

Name: Hisuian Lilligant
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: I suspect that its well-developed legs are the result of a life spent on mountains covered in deep snow. The scent it exudes from its flower crown heartens those in proximity.

Name: Hisuian Lilligant V
Effects: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hisuian Lilligant VSTAR
Ability-Effect: During your turn, you may search your deck for up to 5 in any combination of [G] Pokémon and [G] Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
Effects: You may put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand. If you do, this attack does 100 more damage.
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rowlet
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Flies noiselessly on delicate wings. It has mastered the art of deftly launching dagger-sharp feathers from those same wings.

Name: Dartrix
Flavor-Text: Regularly basks in sunlight to gather power—presumably due to the frigid climate. Nonetheless, the edges of the blade quills set into its wings are keen as ever.

Name: Ponyta
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon live in herds out in the grassland. Newborn foals lack their fiery manes, which will develop about an hour after birth.

Name: Rapidash
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to 150 mph, its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.

Name: Cyndaquil
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Hails from the Johto region. Though usually curled into a ball due to its timid disposition, it harbors tremendous firepower.

Name: Quilava
Flavor-Text: This creature’s fur is most mysterious—it is wholly impervious to the burning touch of flame. Should Quilava turn its back to you, take heed! Such a posture indicates a forthcoming attack.

Name: Heatran V
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
If you have a Stadium in play, this attack does 90 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Heatran VMAX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if you have a Stadium in play, you may heal 50 damage from this Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Radiant Heatran
Effects: This attack does 70 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Boiling blood, like magma, circulates through its body. It makes its dwelling place in volcanic caves.

Name: Psyduck
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Suffers perpetual headaches. If the agony grows too great, Psyduck’s latent power erupts, contrary to Psyduck’s intent. Ergo, I am exploring ways to ease the pain.

Name: Golduck
Effects: Discard each player’s Active Pokémon and all attached cards. (You choose a new Active Pokémon first.)
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in gently flowing rivers. It paddles through the water with its long limbs, putting its graceful swimming skills on display.

Name: Starmie V
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
This attack does 50 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Swinub
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Swinub excels at sniffing out mushrooms buried beneath grass or snow. Since ancient times, the people of Hisui have often relied upon this skill.

Name: Piloswine
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: If it charges at an enemy, the hairs on its back stand up straight. It is very sensitive to sound.

Name: Mamoswine
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can be spotted in wall paintings from as far back as 10,000 years ago. For a while, it was thought to have gone extinct.

Name: Mantine
Effects: Put a Basic Pokémon from either player’s discard pile onto that player’s Bench.
Flavor-Text: This calm and gentle Pokémon swims gracefully through the sea. After building speed, it can leap out of the water. It is often misidentified as a bird Pokémon due to this behavior.

Name: Barboach
Flavor-Text: Makes its home in swamps with murky water. The poor visibility hides this Pokémon from predators, and the slime on its body makes grasping it difficult.

Name: Whiscash
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest at the bottom of swamps. It will eat anything—if it is alive, Whiscash will eat it.

Name: Regice
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon V, it can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: With cold air that can reach temperatures as low as −328 degrees Fahrenheit, Regice instantly freezes any creature that approaches it.

Name: Glaceon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Evolution Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Glaceon is able to lower its body temperature very quickly. It freezes the atmosphere, creating diamond dust that glitters like gems while it flutters and dances around.

Name: Origin Forme Palkia V
Effects: Search your deck for a Stadium card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Origin Forme Palkia VSTAR
Ability-Effect: During your turn, you may attach up to 3 [W] Energy cards from your discard pile to your [W] Pokémon in any way you like. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Oshawott
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon from the Unova region uses the shell on its belly as a weapon to cut down its foes. Thus, I’ve conferred upon this shell the name “scalchop.”.

Name: Dewott
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its exquisite double-scalchop technique is likely the result of daily training, and it can send even masters of the blade fleeing in defeat.

Name: Hisuian Basculin
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Though it differs from other Basculin in several respects, including demeanor—this one is gentle—I have categorized it as a regional form given the vast array of shared qualities.

Name: Hisuian Basculegion
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon during their last turn, this attack does 90 more damage, and your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Clads itself in the souls of comrades that perished before fulfilling their goals of journeying upstream. No other species throughout all Hisui’s rivers is Basculegion’s equal.

Name: Keldeo
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it is resolute, its body fills with power and it becomes swifter. Its jumps are then too fast to follow.

Name: Radiant Greninja
Ability-Effect: You must discard an Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 90 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It appears and vanishes with a ninja’s grace. It toys with its enemies using swift movements, while slicing them with throwing stars of sharpest water.

Name: Bergmite
Flavor-Text: Lives on mountains blanketed in perennial snow. It freezes water vapor in the air to make the ice helmet that it dons for defense.

Name: Hisuian Avalugg
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 120 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium.
Flavor-Text: The armor of ice covering its lower jaw puts steel to shame and can shatter rocks with ease. This Pokémon barrels along steep mountain paths, cleaving through the deep snow.

Name: Galarian Mr. Rime V
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Item cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 90 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Luxray V
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard a Trainer card you find there.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Regieleki
Effects: Put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.
Discard 2 [L] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is a cluster of electrical energy. It’s said that removing the rings on Regieleki’s body will unleash the Pokémon’s latent power.

Name: Hisuian Typhlosion
Ability-Effect: You must discard a [P] Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may make your opponent’s Active Pokémon Burned and Confused.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Said to purify lost, forsaken souls with its flames and guide them to the afterlife. I believe its form has been influenced by the energy of the sacred mountain towering at Hisui’s center.

Name: Hisuian Typhlosion V
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hisuian Typhlosion VSTAR
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has exactly 4 damage counters on it, that Pokémon is Knocked Out. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Togepi
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may heal 10 damage from your Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This ovate creature is frequently mistaken for a moving egg when encountered out in the fields or in the mountains. Its guileless smile soothes the soul.

Name: Togetic
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may heal 30 damage from your Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They say that it will appear before kindhearted, caring people and shower them with happiness.

Name: Togekiss
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may heal 90 damage from your Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon are never seen anywhere near conflict or turmoil. In recent times, they’ve hardly been seen at all.

Name: Misdreavus
Flavor-Text: It conceals itself in darkness, sending chills up travelers’ spines with its childlike weeping. As it observes the frightened travelers with glee, the red orbs upon its chest let offan eerie light.

Name: Mismagius
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. If they have 4 or more cards in their hand, choose all but 3, and your opponent shuffles the chosen cards into their deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its muttered curses can cause awful headaches or terrifying visions that torment others.

Name: Ralts
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Tends to prefer people with a chipper disposition to those who are gloomy, but it has shown no discrimination with regard to age or gender. Needs more research.

Name: Kirlia
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If its Trainer becomes happy, it overflows with energy, dancing joyously while spinning about.

Name: Gallade
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: True to its honorable-warrior image, it uses the blades on its elbows only in defense of something or someone.

Name: Drifloon
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Said to lure away young children and carry them off to the afterlife. Some whisper that Drifloon are formed of reincarnated human souls, but these rumors are as yet unconfirmed.

Name: Drifblim
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Some say this Pokémon is a collection of souls burdened with regrets, silently drifting through the dusk.

Name: Uxie
Effects: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It is said that its emergence gave humans the intelligence to improve their quality of life.

Name: Mesprit
Ability-Effect: If you have Uxie and Azelf in play, your Pokémon have no Weakness.
Flavor-Text: It sleeps at the bottom of a lake. Its spirit is said to leave its body to fly on the lake’s surface.

Name: Azelf
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It is thought that Uxie, Mesprit, and Azelf all came from the same egg.

Name: Diancie
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent plays a Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to your Benched Basic Pokémon.
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: It can instantly create many diamonds by compressing the carbon in the air between its hands.

Name: Wyrdeer
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw a card.
Effects: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The black orbs shine with an uncanny light when the Pokémon is erecting invisible barriers. The fur shed from its beard retains heat well and is a highly useful material for winter clothing.

Name: Hisuian Growlithe
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.
Flavor-Text: They patrol their territory in pairs. I believe the igneous rock components in the fur of this species are the result of volcanic activity in its habitat.

Name: Hisuian Arcanine
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [R] Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage, and your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Snaps at its foes with fangs cloaked in blazing flame. Despite its bulk, it deftly feints every which way, leading opponents on a deceptively merry chase as it all but dances around them.

Name: Machamp V
Effects: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 50 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Machamp VMAX
Effects: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 140 more damage.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use G-Max Chi Strike.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Sudowoodo
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, it has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Though it pretends to be a tree, it fails to fool even children. To the touch, its body feels more like rock than tree bark. Sudowoodo’s extreme aversion to water merits special note.

Name: Regirock
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Every bit of Regirock’s body is made of stone. As parts of its body erode, this Pokémon sticks rocks to itself to repair what’s been lost.

Name: Cranidos
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its hard skull is its distinguishing feature. It snapped trees by headbutting them, and then it fed on their ripe berries.

Name: Rampardos
Effects: If your opponent has 3 or fewer cards in their hand, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This ancient Pokémon used headbutts skillfully. Its brain was really small, so some theories suggest that its stupidity led to its extinction.

Name: Lucario V
Effects: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hippopotas
Flavor-Text: Though large and languid, Hippopotas is difficult to detect due to its tendency to burrow into and lurk beneath the soil. When agitated or excited, it expels sand from its nostrils.

Name: Hippowdon
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Stones can get stuck in the ports on their bodies. Dwebble help dislodge such stones, so Hippowdon look after these Pokémon.

Name: Radiant Hawlucha
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, your Pokémon’s attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon VMAX (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It drives its opponents to exhaustion with its agile maneuvers, then ends the fight with a flashy finishing move.

Name: Hisuian Decidueye
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
This attack does 80 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The air stored inside the rachises of Decidueye’s feathers insulates the Pokémon against Hisui’s extreme cold. This is firm proof that evolution can be influenced by environment.

Name: Hisuian Decidueye V
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hisuian Decidueye VSTAR
Ability-Effect: During your turn, you may draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
Effects: You may discard up to 3 Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 30 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Kleavor
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A violent creature that fells towering trees with its crude axes and shields itself with hard stone. If one should chance upon this Pokémon in the wilds, one’s only recourse is to flee.

Name: Kleavor
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Knocked Out.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A violent creature that fells towering trees with its crude axes and shields itself with hard stone. If one should chance upon this Pokémon in the wilds, one’s only recourse is to flee.

Name: Kleavor V
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hisuian Qwilfish
Flavor-Text: Fishers detest this troublesome Pokémon because it sprays poison from its spines, getting it everywhere. A different form of Qwilfish lives in other regions.

Name: Hisuian Qwilfish
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Fishers detest this troublesome Pokémon because it sprays poison from its spines, getting it everywhere. A different form of Qwilfish lives in other regions.

Name: Hisuian Overqwil
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 5 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.
During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Its lancelike spikes and savage temperament have earned it the nickname “sea fiend.” It slurps up poison to nourish itself.

Name: Hisuian Overqwil
Effects: If you have at least 3 [D] Energy in play, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its lancelike spikes and savage temperament have earned it the nickname “sea fiend.” It slurps up poison to nourish itself.

Name: Hisuian Sneasel
Flavor-Text: Its sturdy, curved claws are ideal for traversing precipitous cliffs. From the tips of these claws drips a venom that infiltrates the nerves of any prey caught in Sneasel’s grasp.

Name: Hisuian Sneasler
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, your Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost is [C][C] less.
Flavor-Text: Because of Sneasler’s virulent poison and daunting physical prowess, no other species could hope to best it on the frozen highlands. Preferring solitude, this species does not form packs.

Name: Hisuian Sneasler V
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
This attack does 80 damage for each Special Condition affecting your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Poochyena
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It has a very tenacious nature. Its acute sense of smell lets it chase a chosen prey without ever losing track.

Name: Mightyena
Ability-Effect: If your opponent has any Pokémon VMAX in play, this Pokémon’s attacks cost [C][C][C] less.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It chases down prey in a pack of around ten. They defeat foes with perfectly coordinated teamwork.

Name: Absol
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Swift as the wind, Absol races through fields and mountains. Its curved, bow-like horn is acutely sensitive to the warning signs of natural disasters.

Name: Darkrai V
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Darkrai VSTAR
Ability-Effect: During your turn, you may put up to 2 Item cards from your discard pile into your hand. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [D] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hisuian Samurott
Ability-Effect: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 3 cards.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Hard of heart and deft of blade, this rare form of Samurott is a product of the Pokémon’s evolution in the region of Hisui. Its turbulent blows crash into foes like ceaseless pounding waves.

Name: Hisuian Samurott V
Effects: Discard up to 2 Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Pokémon.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hisuian Samurott VSTAR
Ability-Effect: During your turn, you may put 4 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 110 more damage.
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Nickit
Flavor-Text: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

Name: Thievul
Ability-Effect: If your opponent has 2 or fewer Prize cards remaining, whenever your opponent plays a Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to your Benched Pokémon V.
Flavor-Text: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

Name: Magnemite
Effects: Shuffle up to 3 [M] Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Flavor-Text: A bizarre Pokémon with but a single eye embedded in an iron sphere. I suspect this creature levitates due to the magnetism it emits from its arms, which resemble horseshoe-shaped magnets.

Name: Magneton
Effects: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is three Magnemite that have linked together. Magneton sends out powerful radio waves to study its surroundings.

Name: Magnezone
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck and attach any number of [M] Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: Some say that Magnezone receives signals from space via the antenna on its head and that it’s being controlled by some mysterious being.

Name: Registeel
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 50 less damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: Registeel’s body is made of a strange material that is flexible enough to stretch and shrink but also more durable than any metal.

Name: Shieldon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Although its fossils can be found in layers of primeval rock, nothing but its face has ever been discovered.

Name: Bastiodon
Ability-Effect: Your Pokémon take 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon V (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is from roughly 100 million years ago. Its terrifyingly tough face is harder than steel.

Name: Bronzor
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Floats using a mysterious energy. The pattern engraved upon its back is held as sacred and can sometimes be found in imagery from ancient cemeteries and other such timeworn places.

Name: Bronzong
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s [R] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Some believe it to be a deity that summons rain clouds. When angered, it lets out a warning cry that rings out like the tolling of a bell.

Name: Origin Forme Dialga V
Effects: Attach up to 2 [M] Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Origin Forme Dialga VSTAR
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Take another turn after this one. (Skip Pokémon Checkup.) (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pawniard
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It uses river stones to maintain the cutting edges of the blades covering its body. These sharpened blades allow it to bring down opponents.

Name: Bisharp
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched Pawniard.
Flavor-Text: It’s accompanied by a large retinue of Pawniard. Bisharp keeps a keen eye on its minions, ensuring none of them even think of double-crossing it.

Name: Garchomp V
Effects: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 220 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Regidrago
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may draw cards until you have 4 cards in your hand. You can’t use more than 1 Dragon’s Hoard Ability each turn.
Flavor-Text: An academic theory proposes that Regidrago’s arms were once the head of an ancient dragon Pokémon. The theory remains unproven.

Name: Eevee
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play a Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your other Eevee, you may search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.

Name: Hoothoot
Ability-Effect: Basic Energy attached to your Benched Pokémon can’t be discarded by an effect of your opponent’s Item or Supporter cards.
Flavor-Text: It always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.

Name: Noctowl
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its eyes are specially developed to enable it to see clearly even in murky darkness and minimal light.

Name: Teddiursa
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, put an Item card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It licks its paws because of the sweet honey that has soaked into them. It is cunning, stealing into the nests of Combee and taking for itself the honey that the Combee have amassed.

Name: Ursaring
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: When the cold season arrives in Hisui, this Pokémon will wander fields and mountains alike in search of its favorite berries. Ursaring’s hunger during this time makes it a ferocious danger.

Name: Ursaluna
Effects: Put up to 2 cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: I believe it was Hisui’s swampy terrain that gave Ursaluna its burly physique and newfound capacity to manipulate peat at will.

Name: Stantler
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its strangely shaped antlers have the power to bewitch those who see them. Medicine made by grinding up the black orbs from fallen antlers is an effective treatment for insomnia.

Name: Miltank
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon V.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Miltank produces highly nutritious milk, so it’s been supporting the lives of people and other Pokémon since ancient times.

Name: Glameow
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Bewitches humans with its helical tail and piercing gaze. Its hidden claws are quite sharp as well, making this Pokémon an exceedingly tricky opponent if antagonized.

Name: Purugly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: To make itself appear intimidatingly beefy, it tightly cinches its waist with its twin tails.

Name: Chatot
Effects: Draw a card.
During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: A versatile performer skilled in the imitation of human speech. It is said that older, more experienced Chatot can even understand the meaning of the words they mimic.

Name: Regigigas
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if you have Regirock, Regice, Registeel, Regieleki, and Regidrago in play, you may attach up to 3 Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon VMAX, this attack does 150 more damage.
Flavor-Text: There is an enduring legend that states this Pokémon towed continents with ropes.

Name: Rufflet
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Its chick-like looks belie its hotheadedness. It challenges its parents at every opportunity, desperate to prove its strength.

Name: Hisuian Braviary
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Slashing Strike.
